title: At the Mountains of Madness by H. P. Lovecraft
author: H. P. Lovecraft
exclude_reason: The text is incomplete—it is explicitly truncated ('[... text truncated ...]') and ends mid-sentence, so it does not read as a complete standalone narrative. It also contains an embedded Project Gutenberg transcriber's note and duplicated/garbled passages.
summary: A geologist recounts the Miskatonic University Antarctic expedition, during which the discovery of ancient star-headed "Elder Things" leads to the massacre of a research party and revelations of a monstrous prehuman civilization amid the mountains of madness. He tells the tale to dissuade a future expedition from unleashing nameless horrors.

issues.description: Project Gutenberg transcriber's note embedded at the start of the story body: '[Transcriber's Note: This etext was produced from Astounding Stories February, March, April 1936...]'

The story text is truncated with an explicit '[... text truncated ...]' marker and ends mid-sentence ('It was, very clearly, the b'). The narrative lacks a proper ending and is not a complete standalone text.

Several passages appear duplicated/garbled, likely extraction artifacts (e.g., a paragraph about 'queer state of sensations... pinnacles' repeated, and 'The effect of the monstrous sight was indescribable!' restated). Also stray isolated quote fragments inserted after Lake's messages.
Byline 'By H. P. LOVECRAFT' present at start of story body.

title: Cool Air by H. P. Lovecraft
author: H. P. Lovecraft
exclude_reason: Complete, correctly-genred horror story, but contains an embedded transcriber's note with copyright statement and a duplicated pull-quote line that should be cleaned before inclusion.
summary: A narrator befriends the reclusive, invalid Doctor Muñoz, who maintains his chilled apartment through ammonia refrigeration; when the cooling machine fails, it is revealed the doctor had been artificially preserving his body since dying eighteen years earlier, and he dissolves into decay.

issues.description: Project Gutenberg/Weird Tales transcriber's note embedded near the start: '[Transcriber's Note: This etext was produced from Weird Tales September 1939...]' including copyright-renewal statement.
A sentence is duplicated as a stray quoted pull-quote/caption: 'He groped his way out with face tightly bandaged, and I never saw his eyes again.' appears twice in succession.
Title heading 'Cool Air' and byline 'By H. P. LOVECRAFT' plus an editorial tagline appear at the start (flagged by pre-flight QA).

title: He by H. P. Lovecraft
author: H. P. Lovecraft
exclude_reason: Story is complete, well-formed, and clearly horror, but contains an embedded transcriber's note at the end that would need removal for a clean corpus.
summary: A disillusioned poet wandering nocturnal New York meets a strange cloaked man who leads him to an ancient house and reveals supernatural visions of past and future; the vision-magic summons a vengeful spectral horde that destroys the man and his house, leaving the narrator to flee back to New England.

issues.description: A transcriber's note appears at the end of the story body: 'Transcriber's Note: This story appeared in the September 1926 issue of Weird Tales Magazine.'

title: The Call of Cthulhu by H. P. Lovecraft
author: H. P. Lovecraft
exclude_reason: Complete, correctly-genred classic horror story, but the body contains an embedded transcriber's note and title/byline heading contamination that warrant cleanup before inclusion.
summary: The narrator pieces together his late grand-uncle's research, a New Orleans police raid, and a Norwegian sailor's manuscript to uncover the worldwide cult of Cthulhu — a monstrous cosmic entity dreaming in the sunken city of R'lyeh — and comes to fear he knows too much to survive.

issues.description: A Project Gutenberg/Weird Tales transcriber's note appears embedded near the start of the story body: '[Transcriber's Note: This etext was produced from Weird Tales, February 1928...]'.
Start-of-story contamination flagged: the title heading 'The CALL of CTHULHU' and byline 'By H.P. LOVECRAFT' are included in the story body.

title: The Case of Charles Dexter Ward by H. P. Lovecraft
author: H. P. Lovecraft
summary: Antiquarian Charles Dexter Ward discovers his descent from the sinister long-lived sorcerer Joseph Curwen and, delving into occult research and grave-searching, revives ancestral necromantic horrors. The provided text is truncated before the story's conclusion.

issues.description: The story text is explicitly truncated ('[... text truncated ...]') and cuts off mid-sentence ('For '), so there is no ending. This is only a portion of Lovecraft's novel-length work.
Embedded Project Gutenberg / Weird Tales transcriber note at the start: '[Transcriber's Note: This etext was produced from Weird Tales May, July 1941 ...]'.
Copyright-renewal statement embedded in the transcriber note regarding U.S. copyright not being renewed.
Promotional publisher blurb inserted at the start ('Here is THE CASE OF CHARLES DEXTER WARD—the last, and many think the best... So just turn the page—and on with the show!').
A 'CONTENTS' heading appears in the body, along with numbered chapter/section headings ('1. A Result and a Prologue', '2. An Antecedent and a Horror', '3. A Search and an Evocation').
A stray caption line duplicating story text appears in quotes: '"They found the grave vacant—precisely as they had expected."'
